n. f. From H. pair foot. A ladder; staircase; flight of steps; a step of a ladder.
(from پير) s. f. An ornament worn on the legs.

def: s.f. Ladder, stair-case, flight of steps, stairs; step or rung of a ladder; — the track or path of oxen (in drawing water, &c. = paiṛā; pair, q.v.).

ety: pair˚ = Prk. पइर(इ) = S. प्रतिर(ति), rt. प्र+तॄ
def: v.n. To swim; to float.

ety: i.e. pāʼī or pāʼi+sā; S. पाद+इका+सदृशकः
def: s.m. A copper coin, a pice (the fourth part of an ānā; in value about a farthing and a half); — money, cash, wealth, riches, fortune
more: s. m. 1. The name of a copper coin. 2. Money, cash. پيسااڙانا paisā uṛānā, 1. To spend extravagantly. 2. To take the money of another by theft or deceit. پيساپهيرپهارکرنا paisā pher phār karnā, To turn the penny. پيساڐبونا paisā ḍubonā, To sink money, to lay out money without return. پيساڐوبنا paisā ḍūbnā, To be sunk (money). پيساکهانا paisā khānā, 1. To spend extravagantly, to waste. 2. To subsist by wages or the produce of labour. 3. To take bribes. 4. To embezzle. پيسي سون درباربندنا paise soṅ darbār bandnā, To bribe, give bribes. پيسي لگانا paise lagānā, To lay out or expend money. پيسي والا paise-wālā, adj. 1. Moneyed. 2. Worth one paisā.
s. m. A copper coin in value about two farthings ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: (-kā), To spend money extravagantly; — to make away with the money (of another); to take the money (of another) by theft or deceit
v.n. To sink money, to lay out money without return
v.n. Money to be sunk or lost
(-kā), To spend extravagantly, to waste; — to subsist by wages or the produce (of labour); — to embezzle; to take bribes
(meṅ), To lay out or expend money (upon)
adj. & s.m. Moneyed, wealthy; — worth one paisā; — a moneyed man.

def: n. m. S. पण 1. A copper coin, in value about two farthings; pice. Chār damṛī kā paisā! 2. Money; wealth; riches; fortune. 1. Iske pās baṛā paisā hai . 2. Paisā ke tāt, māt, paisā ke hitu, yār, paise kī bahan, bhāṅj, paise kī lugaīyā hai, Paise soṅ ādar, sat-kār hot, panchan meṅ paise bin yār koū bāt na puchhaiyā hai, Paisā soṅ dharm, karm; bharm sab paisā soṅ, paisā sūṅ chalī jāt paṭpar par naīyā hai, Kaheṅ Rāmdatt, 'are, chet re achet bhoṅdhū, Bidh ne banāyo baṛā jagat meṅ rupaīyā hai . Kabit. 'Tis pice makes father, mother, friend, 'tis pice makes sister, niece, and wife, 'Tis pice 'mong men respect commands, piceless may pass uncared your life, Alms, rites, and credit come from pice, pice will your boat on dry land draw, Awake, thou Fool! coin greatest God hath made on earth a law. pais'yal, paise-wālā, paisalchī , adj. Worth one paisā. paise-wālā , n. m. A rich man; a man of fortune.
adj. & s.m. = paiśāć, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to a piśāća or demon; infernal, demoniacal; — the eighth and lowest form of marriage (described as taking place when a lover secretly embraces a maiden either sleeping or intoxicated or disordered in her intellect); — a pisāća or demon.
s.f. A sort of gibberish put into the mouths of demons by the dramatic poets; one of the lowest forms of Prākr̤it dialects.
adj. Worth one paisa ( = paise-wālā).
adj. (dakh.) (One) having money.
adj. (dakh.) Rich, moneyed.
s. f. (dakh.) Richness, ability, means.
s.m. Reach, compass, extent; access, entrance, admission; ability, power.
adj. = paiṅsaṭh, q.v. adj. Sixty-five.

adj. Worth one paisā ( = paise-wālā).
adj. (f. -ī), Shallow.

s. m. Flourishing about before cudgelling, &c.
subs: v.n. To go through the usual attitudes and flourishes before closing (with an antagonist).

more: (caus. of پيٿهنا q. v.) To force in, to make to penetrate, to thrust, to run in, to introduce, to insinuate.

def: v.t. = paiṭhālnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to penetrate; to cause to pervade; to thrust (in, -meṅ), force (in); to run (in), to introduce (into); to insinuate.

def: v.t. To cause to penetrate; to cause to pervade; to thrust (in, -meṅ), force (in); to run (in), to introduce (into); to insinuate.

v.t. To allow (one) to enter, to give entrance (to), to admit

def: v. n. 1. To enter; force an entry; drive in; break in; run into. Jin ḍhūṅḍā tin pāīyāṅ gahre pānī paiṭh; Maiṅ baurī ḍūban ḍarī, rahī kināre baiṭh . Dohā. Who in deep waters plunged and sought have found, but foolish me sat by the shore through fear of being drowned. 2. To intrude; encroach; trespass. 3. To infiltrate; impregnate; impregn; fecundate. paiṭhne denā , v. n. To let in; give entrance to; admit; receive. paiṭhne-wālā , n. m. 1. Intruder; trespasser. 2. A diver; one who cleans out wells.
adj. Of or belonging to ancestors, or progenitors, or to the manes; paternal, ancestral, inherited from a father, hereditary
more: adj. Hereditary, inherited from a father.
subs: s.f. A paternal estate; the country of one's ancestors, fatherland.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Pertaining to the bilious humour; proceeding from bile; bilious.
more: n. f. S. पृष्ट, Pr. puṭṭhi; P. pusht . 1. The back; the loins; the back part. 2. Backing; support; assistance; help. pīṭh par kā , n. m. pīṭh par kī , n. f. A younger child. pīṭh par honā , v. n. To back; support; aid; help. pīṭh pīchhe , adv. Behind; in one's back; in the absence of. 1. Pīṭh pīchhe bādshāh ko burā kahte haiṅ . 2. Pīṭh pīchhe kuchh hī ho. pīṭh pīchhe kahnā , v. n. To backbite. pīṭh ṭhoknā , v. a. To pat on the shoulder; to say bravo; to back; encourage. pīṭh chārpāī se lag jānā , v. n. To be bedridden. pīṭh denā, pīṭh dikhānā, yā moṛnā , v. n. 1. To show one's back; run away; turn tail. Chhatrī kā yeh dharm nahīṅ bhāg dikhāve pīṭh; Yā māre, yā mar rahe, lipaṭ jāë jūṅ kīṭ . Chaubolā. 'Tis not the Kshatri's wont in battle e'er his back to show. To do or die his cue—like black ant grip, nor leave his foe. 2. To turn, shrink or recoil from; funk. 3. To depart; set out on a j…

E.; n. f. S. प्रवेश to enter. 1. Ingress; entrance; admission. 2. Penetration; infiltration; impregnation; sexual penetration. 3. A duplicate bill of exchange. par-paiṭh , n. f. A triplicate bill of exchange.

def: adj. Of a bilious temperament, bilious; biliary.

part. adj. (used in comp.), Joined, connected; joining, connecting, &c. ( = paiwasta); — s.m. Junction, conjunction, addition; connection, relation; relationship; — joint, join, piece, patch; graft; bud
more: n. m. A patch; join; graft; bud. Lagāiye, lajāiye; lagāë binā sare nahīṅ; dhan haiṅ unke bhāg jinke lagte nahīṅ . Riddle. Ans. Paivand a patch. To apply it one's ashamed, unless applied it will not wear, Happy the state of those who've no such anxious care whate'er. pevand-kārī , n. f. Patching; cobbling; grafting. pevand lagānā , v. a. To patch; add a piece; graft.

1. part. in compos. (of پيوستن) Joining, joined, connecting, connected. 2. s. m. Junction, conjunction, addition, connexion, relationship, a joint. 3. A patch, a graft, a bud, a scion. پيوندکار paiwand-kār, s. A patcher, grafter. پيوندکاري paiwand-kār-ī, s. f. Patching, cobbling. پيوندلگانا paiwand lagānā, To patch, to graft, to inoculate (a tree).
subs: (-kā), To join (to), to make (a person or thing) a piece (of); to bury (the word zamīn being expressed, e.g. mujhe jītā-hī zamīn-kā paiwand kar-do)
(-meṅ), To put a piece (in or on); to add a piece (to), to patch; to graft; to inoculate (a tree)
(-kā), To be joined (to), to become a piece, part or parcel (of); to be interred (e.g. jab we zamīn-kā paiwand ho-jāweṅ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پَیوَنْد (paywand).

def: adj. & s.m. Patched, pieced; grafted, engrafted; — an engrafted tree; — a hybrid, mongrel; a bastard; — a peach
more: adj. Grafted; engrafted. paivandī , n. m. 1. A hybrid; mongrel; cross-breed; half caste; bastard. 2. A peach ( āṛū ). Ḍālyoṅ kā ghulā hī paivandī hai! Delhi street cry. pevandī mūṅchheṅ , n. f. Whiskers that reach the beard, the ends of which are twisted in the form of a circle on the cheek ( gal-muchchheṅ ).
adj. Ingrafted. پيوندي بير paiwandī ber, s. m. An ingrafted ber (Zizyphus jujuba). پيوندي موچهين paiwandī mūchheṅ, s. f. The whiskers joining the beard.
subs: s.f. A patched covering or gabardine
s.f. Whiskers that join and are twisted in the beard in the form of a circle on the cheek (syn. gal-muććheṅ).

more: (from پيوستن) 1. s. f. Junction, connexion, friendship. 2. part. Joined.

ety: part. of paiwastan; and = Zend paiti+basta; S. प्रतिबद्ध
def: part. adj. Joined, contiguous, adjacent, touching, sticking close, firmly united, inseparable; absorbed; successive, continued without interruption; — adv. Always, continually, uninterruptedly; — s.m. The year before last
more: adj. See پچّي 1. Joined to; contiguous. 2. Absorbed; sucked in; inseparable ( ek-jigar ). 3. Closely penetrated; close-fitted; driven in; firm.
1. adj. Joined, contiguous. 2. adv. Always, continually. 3. s. m. The year before last. پيوسته ابرو paiwasta-abrū, adj. Whose eyebrows join above the nose.
subs: adj. & s.m. Having the eyebrows joined; — one whose eyebrows meet.

def: s.f. Junction, connection, union, adhesion, contiguity, closeness, coherence, continuity; close intimacy, attachment, friendship.
more: s. f. Junction, connexion, adhesion, contiguity, closeness, attachment.

s. Autumn: see پاييز pāyīz.

s. f. A slipper, a shoe. پيزاراپيزاري paizārā-paizārī, s. f. Fighting or mutual beating with slippers. پيزارپٿي ياکاري paizār-paṭṭī or -kārī, Act of beating with slippers. پيزارخور paizār-ḵẖor, adj. Abject, mean, base (q. d. fit or deserving to be beaten with slippers). پيزارسي paizār se, An expression of contempt, signifying it concerns not or affects not me, I care not. پيزارکهانا paizār khānā, To be beaten with slippers.

v.n. To suffer a shoe-beating, be beaten with slippers.

adj. 1. Pure; clear; clean; spotless; undefiled; unpolluted. 2. Sacred; holy; consecrated; hallowed. Pāk nām Allāh kā . Prov. God is pure. 3. Purified; washed; swept clean; cleansed. Khas kam, jahāṅ pāk . Prov. The less scum, the purer earth. (Said of the death of a bad man.) 4. Unmixed; unalloyed; unadulterated. 5. Blameless; guiltless; innocent; honest. Pāk rah, be-bāk rah . Prov. Be pure and fear not. 6. Free; exempt; free from (debt). 7. ( Ironic .) Unmixed; perfect. Pāk shohdah. pāk-bāz , n. m. ( Bhangaṛs' idiom) A filter. pāk-dāman ; H. sītā-satī , adj. 1. Chaste; virtuous; modest. 2. Pure; innocent; holy. pāk-sāf , adj. Pure; clean; undefiled; unpolluted. pāk karnā , v. a. 1. To purify; clarify; clear; strain. 2. To sift; winnow; wash. pāk mohabbat , n. f. Pure or Platonic love. pāk nīyat , n. f. Pure intention or motive; good faith.
s. m. An electuary medicine, confection.
adj. Pure, clean, holy, innocent, spotless, inmaculate, fair.

def: n. f. S. प्रग्रह to gain. 1. The act of seizing; seizure; hold. Ūṅṭ kī pakaṛ, kutte kī jhaj aṭ . Prov. From a camel's hold, and a dog's spring keep clear. 2. Grasp; reach; handle. 3. Apprehension; capture ( giriftārī ). 4. Fighting; wrestling ( kushtī ). Āo pakaṛ kī ṭhaire . 5. Gain; profit. Kachaihrī meṅ roz do chār rupaë kī pakaṛ ho jātī hai . 6. Taking one up ( girift ); criticism. Hamāri bātoṅ kī pakaṛ kartā hai. pakṛā jānā , v. n. 1. To be apprehended, arrested. 2. To be found out, caught in the act.
s.m. The waved-leaved fig-tree, Ficus infectoria (syn. gūlaṛ).

def: v.t. To catch; to lay hold of, to hold, take, seize, clutch, gripe, handle; to capture, apprehend; to come up to, to overtake; to catch tripping, to detect, find out, discover; to object, to criticise, find fault (with), pick holes (in), to carp, cavil; to acquire, get, gain, receive

wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀧𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀟𑁆- (*pakkaḍ-). Cognate to Gujarati પકડવું (pakaḍvũ), Marathi पकडणे (pakaḍṇe). Further etymology is uncertain, but it may derive from Sanskrit प्रकृष्टयति (prakṛṣṭayati, “to catch”) or प्रगृह्णाति (pragṛhṇāti, “to hold”). The former is from प्र- (pra-, “pro-”) + कृष्टयति (kṛṣṭayati), from कृष् (kṛṣ, “to draw, lead, plough”), and the latter from प्र- (pra-, “pro-”) + गृह्णा…

def: v. a. 1. To catch hold of; clutch; grapple; handle. Chandal-pur meṅ chori kīyā, aur Chuṭṭakpur meṅ pakṛānā, Hattal-pur meṅ insāf huā, Nawal-pur meṅ marrānā . Riddle. Ans. Jūṅ louse. In crown-head town he stole, 'tween finger's tips was caught, On the palm he'd trial fair, on the nails to die his lot. 2. To seize; capture; apprehend; arrest. Chhināl ko pakṛe khāṭ se, chor ko pakṛe gāṅṭh se . Prov. The adultress is caught in bed, the thief with his bundle. 3. To keep; keep in hand; hold tight; hold fast. 4. To come up to or with; reach (pahuṅchnā) ; overtake. 1. Meṅh ne pakaṛ līyā . 2. Jis din kuchh kām nahīṅ hotā shām pakaṛnī dush- wār ho jātī hai . 5. To find out; detect; discover; hit upon. Pulis ne pān sau kī chorī pakṛī . 6. To get; earn; gain, acquire. Chār āne pakṛe. Kharbūze ko dekh-kar kharbūzā raṅg pakaṛtā hai . Prov. Melons from melons seen their color take. (Society moulds men). 7. To attack; seize; possess; come on. Gaṭhyā ne pakaṛ liyā . 8. To press into one's service; to press. Begār pakaṛnā . 9. To criticize; carp; cavil; pick holes; find fault. 10. To stick to ( jamnā ); adhere; take. Divāl ne chūnā pakaṛ līyā .
Caus. of پکرَنا v. a.
v.t. To cause to seize; to get (one) to lay hold of; to cause to be seized, &c. ( = pakṛānā, q.v.).
more: (caus. of پکڙنا) v. a. To cause to seize.

Chār uṅgal kā peṛ, savā man kā pattā, Phal lage alag, alag, pak jāë ikhaṭṭā . Riddle. Ans. Kumhār kā chāk . The potter's wheel. ( Met .) To be consumed, burnt, roasted. Terī bātoṅ se kalejā pak gayā . Wom. 5. To be completed, made up, made good. 6. To be settled; fixed; decided. Bāt pak gaī . 7. To be struck (a bargain). Saudā pak gayā . 8. To plot; scheme; mature a plan. 9. ( Chausar ) To be about to win. Nard pak gaī . pakā pakāyā , adj. Cooked and ready. pak'āū , adj. Suppurative. pakāo , n. m. Suppuration.

def: s.f. The taking up a side or argument; partisanship, alliance.
s.f. The root or insertion of a bird's wing; the first day of the waxing or waning of the moon.

adj. & s.m. Winged; taking the side (of), siding (with); — a bird; an arrow; a partisan, favourer, ally, assistant, advocate, patron; — pakshi-rāj, s.m. 'King of the birds,' an epithet of Garuḍa; — the peacock
more: n. m. 1. A bird; the feathered race. 2. Partisan; advocate; ally; patron. pakshi-rāj , n. m. lit . King of the birds. An epithet of Gaṛuṛ , hence the peacock.
subs: s.f. The form or condition of a bird or of a beast.
wety: From Sanskrit पक्षिन् (pakṣin).

def: s.f. A hen bird; a female partisan; a night with the days preceding and following it.
adj. Belonging to a side or party.
s.m. Violent pain or inflammation of the bowels (arising from indigestion), colic.
adj. Cooked, &c. = pakkā, q.v.; — digested; ripe for destruction; on the eve of decay.
s.f. Price paid for cooking.
n. f. The price paid for cooking.
s. f. Price paid for cooking.
s.m. 'Cooked or dressed food'; sweetmeat or cake, &c. fried in butter or oil (such as pūrī, gulgulā, &c.).
more: n. m. Pastry; sweetmeats. 1. Bhūk meṅ gūlar pakvān . Prov. Hunger doth greet wild figs as dainties sweet. 2. Mere mīyāṅ ko bhūk jo lāge kyā re karūṅ pakvān? Piste, badām kī roṭī pakātī, hirnī ke kartī kabāb! Lorī . Lullaby. My lord is hungry, what nice things for him I'll make! Pistachio, almond cakes and venison broiled steak!
s. m. Sweetmeats, victuals fried in butter or oil.
wety: From پکانا (“to cook”).

def: v.t. To cause to cook or dress, to get (one) to cook, &c. ( = pakānā); to cause to be cooked or dressed, to have (a thing) cooked (by); to cause to ripen.
more: v. a. Caus. of پکانا q. v.
(caus. of پکنا) v. a. To cause to be dressed or cooked, to cause to ripen.

def: s.m. A kind of medicinal salt, impregnated with iron (syn. biṭlavan); saltpetre; nitre.

def: s.m. A particular weight (equal to four karshas or the one hundredth part of a tola); a particular measure of time, a period of twenty-four seconds; a second, instant, moment; — flesh; straw
more: n. m. S. पल to move. The twinkling of an eye; a moment; instant. The 60th part of a ghaṛī or hour of 24 minutes, and consequently equivalent to 24 seconds. 2½ pals equal one minute. pal pal, pal pal meṅ , adv. Every moment; every minute. Āshiq usī ko jānye jo ahl-i-dard ho, Pal pal meṅ ṭhanḍe sāṅs le, aur raṅg zard ho . A lover true is he who hath a feeling heart, Pale-faced, each moment heaved a deep sigh from his heart. ek pal meṅ, pal bhar meṅ , adv. In the twinkling of an eye; in an instant.
s. m. A moment, the 60th part of a ghaṛī or danḍ (2 1/2 pals being equal to one minute).
contract. of پلک palak, q. v. پل اوٿ pal-oṭ, For an instant. پل مارنا pal-mārnā, To make a motion of the eyelid. پل مارتي pal mārte, adv. In the twinkling of an eye, immediately, instantly.
subs: (pala+ada), s.m.f. 'Flesh-eater,' a rākshas, goblin, (male or female)
ek pal-meṅ, adv. In a moment or instant, in the twinkling of an eye
pal-pal-meṅ, adv. Every instant, every moment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پل (pul).

def: s.f. The eyelid; a moment
subs: adv. In the twinkling of an eye, instantly, immediately
v.n. To make a movement of the eyelid, to wink
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پل (pul).

def: s.m. Shelter; protection; crown lands kept in the hands of government.
more: n. m. S. पाल् to protect. 1. A dam; dyke; bank; an embankment to confine the water for irrigation. 2. A small tent. 3. A sail. 4. (S. पलाल, , H. puāl straw) Layers of straw, leaves, etc. between which unripe mangoes, etc. are ripened. 1. Pāl khā, ṭapke ka mazā dekh . Street cry. 2. Hotā hai shīrīṅ to bahut pāl kā, Lek hai ṭapke kā bhī turfā mazā . Green mangoes in hot-houses ripened are most sweet, So too are mellow mangoes plucked a luscious treat. 5. Crown lands kept in the hands of Government ( khālsā ). pāl khānā , E. v. n. To copulate; to pair (as birds).
s. m. f. 1. A sail. 2. A small tent. 3. Layers of straw, leaves, &c. between which unripe mangos are ripened.
(perhaps, from पाल Line, range) s. (dakh.) A dam, dike, bank ( Harris ): see مينڐ menḍ.
wety: Borrowed from English Paul.

def: s.m. Frost, hoar-frost; snow; dew; coagulation; secundines of an animal
more: n. m. 1. A heap of earth, etc. set up by boys to separate the two parties in the game of kabaḍḍī ; a line of demarcation; partition line; battle field. 2. Power; sway; authority; clutches. 3. Dealing; business; transaction. Ham woh miyāṅ haiṅ—Allah pālā na ḍāle ham se, Rahte haiṅ hāth bāṅdhe ab husn-wāle ham se . Nazīr. I'm such a killing beau—Heaven save you from my power— The greatest beauties suppliant stand 'fore me each hour. 4. (S. पलाल straw). The leaves of the ber tree. pālā, yā pāle paṛnā , v. n. 1. To fall under one's power; to have to do with. 1. Khudā zālim se pālā na ḍāle! Prov. God save me from the tyrant's power! 2. Achchhe ghar meṅ lā-ḍālā, kaisī kīnī Haq-tālā? Woh bhaṅg pië rahe matwālā, aise se paṛā merā pālā . Song. Lāonī . A fine house I have been thrown in, my God, what hast thou done! He bhaṅg imbibes and drunken lies; to such a one tied down! 2. To be married to, b…
n. m. S. प्रालेय Frost; hoarfrost; snow. Ab-ke nāj ko pālā mār gayā. pālā paṛnā, girnā , v. n. See os paṛnā , (1, 2). To snow; to fall (snow).

s. m. 1. Frost, hoar-frost, snow. 2. Trust, charge. 3. A heap of earth made by children to separate the two parties in a game called kabaḍḍī. 4. Leaves of a tree named jhaṛberī (a species of Zizyphus). پالاپڙنا pālā paṛnā, v. n. To snow. پالي پڙنا pāle paṛnā, v. n. To fall within the power of another.
subs: pālā girnā, v.n. To freeze, to snow.

def: s.f. The eyelid; eyelash; — a moment, instant ( = pal)
more: n. f. 1. The eyelashes ( papnī ). Man le gaī chanchal mār palak, Dikhlā-ke tanak nainoṅ kī jhalak . Song. Ṭhumrī . One glance the sprightly sylph shot from her eye, A lightning flash—my heart was gone for aye. 2. A moment ( pal ). 1. Kakkā, kaṅwal-naiṅ jab te gaë, jab te chit nahiṅ Biyākul jal bin mīn, yoṅ palak na lāgat nain . Bārah-khāṛī. Alphabet rhyme. 2. Mujh se woh kahte haiṅ, dāman ko jhaṭak 'sone de;' 'Be-adab, chhoṛ de, bas ek palak sone de .' Rangin.

s. f. m. The eyelid; a moment. پلک دريا palak-daryā, s. m. Clouds. پلک مارنايامٿکانا pulak-mārnā or -maṭkānā, v. a. To wink. پلک لگنا palak lagnā, Twinkling of the eye, closing of the eyelids. پلک مين palak meṅ or پلک کي مارتي palak ke mārte, In the twinkling of an eye.
subs: v.n. Closing of the eyelids; twinkling of the eye
palāk mārnā, v.n. To wink, twinkle
palak-ke mārte, adv. In the twinkling of an eye, in a moment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پلک (palk, palak).
